headlines.title: DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin threatens states over elections after Trump speech
headlines.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin on Friday threatened state election officials.

headlines.title: Democrat moves to drop out of Nebraska’s Senate race, clearing the path for an independent
headlines.description: Nebraska Democratic Senate nominee Cindy Burbank has submitted a letter requesting her name be removed from the ballot, a move that would set up a one-on-one matchup between Republican Sen.

headlines.title: Trump backs Sen. Darline Graham in primary to replace her late brother
headlines.description: Trump backed Sen. Darline Graham, R-S.C., in the special election to fill her late brother’s Senate seat for a full term.

headlines.title: Americans should have ‘confidence’ in accuracy of 2020 election: Ousted election commissioner
headlines.description: NBC News correspondents break down President Trump's address to the nation on election integrity and allegations that the U.S. election system is insecure, including accusations against China. Thomas Hicks, who was recently fired as chair of the Election Assistance Commission, reacts to the president’s claims.

headlines.title: Trump Uses Prime-Time Speech to Revive False Election Claims
headlines.description: President Donald Trump used his prime-time speech Thursday to raise questions about election integrity in the build-up to November’s key midterms while reviving false claims about the 2020 election and asserting that China targeted his campaign. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: How Trump's election allegations against China could impact relations with U.S.
headlines.description: President Trump accused China of having a role in influencing the 2020 election during his speech about election interference. NBC News' Garrett Haake has more on how this could impact China's relationship with the U.S.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Wisconsin Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez drops out of the governor’s race
headlines.description: Wisconsin Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez said Friday that she was dropping out of the governor’s race, just days after her campaign discovered major inaccuracies related to its financial reporting.

headlines.title: Federal panel signals it could support permanent fencing around White House park known for protests
headlines.description: A historic hub for free speech and protest is under the spotlight as the Trump administration proposes adding permanent fencing around Lafayette Square.

headlines.title: 3 big Senate fundraising takeaways: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, we break down the toplines from a fresh batch of campaign finance filings. Plus, Jonathan Allen previews President Donald Trump’s speech tonight from the White House.

headlines.title: American woman held in Iran since 2024 released, Trump announces
headlines.description: Jared Genser, a lawyer who has been involved with high-profile cases of U.S. citizens detained in Iran, named the released citizen as his client, Dena Karari.

headlines.title: Sen. John Fetterman says he’ll leave the Democratic Party if it ‘officially’ becomes ‘anti-Israel’
headlines.description: Sen. John Fetterman said he would leave the Democratic Party if it “officially” becomes “the anti-Israel party.”
